name: Navy Bean Soup Cuban Style
recipeIngredient: Navy beans (dried) or 3 cans of great northern beans (15 oz each)
Salt pork (2 oz, rind removed, cut into small pieces)
Butternut squash (1/2 lb, seeds removed, cut into chunks)
recipeInstructions:
1. If using dried navy beans, rinse them in cold water and pick over to remove foreign particles. Soak the beans covered in water overnight.
2. Drain the beans and place them in a large saucepan with 2.5 quarts of water, bay leaves, and salt pork. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, covered, until tender (1-1.5 hours), adding water if necessary. If using canned beans, skip this step as they are already cooked.
3. In a medium skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Sauté onion, green pepper, garlic, oregano, salt pork, and tomato sauce for 10 minutes.
4. Boil squash chunks in a separate saucepan for 5 minutes.
5. When the beans are tender, add the tomato mixture, potato, salt, pepper, and squash to the beans. Continue cooking another 30 minutes.
6. Remove and discard the salt pork.
7. Serve the soup in bowls. Serves 6.
Note: This recipe is based on The Columbia Restaurant Spanish Cookbook by Judi M. Phelps.
